title: Refactor a non-idempotent invoice worker using an idempotency ledger
prompt: The worker in `test_fixtures/non_idempotent_worker.ex` charges a customer and sends a receipt on every retry. Even after adding a `charged_at` timestamp check, there's still a race: if `charge_card` succeeds but the process crashes before `mark_charged`, a retry will double-charge. Rewrite using the idempotency-ledger pattern: (1) perform/1 takes `%{"invoice_id" => id, "idempotency_key" => key}` (the key is generated at enqueue time), (2) before charging, check `MyApp.Billing.find_charge_attempt(key)` — if a record exists with status `:succeeded`, return `:ok`; if `:pending`, proceed with the charge; (3) mark the attempt as `:pending` BEFORE calling the external API, then `:succeeded` after, (4) use `Ecto.Multi` to wrap the mark_pending and charge together. Write to `lib/my_app/workers/invoice_worker.ex`.
tier_rationale: Full idempotency-ledger pattern including the mark-pending-first ordering. Multi-hop reasoning about crash windows and retry semantics.
research_citation: DZone: 'Idempotency in AI Tools: The Most Expensive Mistake' — 'A network timeout during a large language model API call that triggers a retry can result in paying twice.'
